document.title: Vector Loom Document Format (v1)
document.purpose: Define the v1 JSON data format for 2-D nestable vector structures used by the Vector Loom runtime and editor.
author: Lion Kimbro
Claude Code

orientation.summary: A Vector Loom document describes a tree of 2-D vector structures: primitives (line, rect, oval, polyline, text), markers (port), grouping with local coordinate systems (group), and reusable structures placed by reference (defs + instance). The format is a semantic visual-object format, not merely a list of draw commands: structures have identity (ids), local coordinate systems, and named connector points so they can attach to one another and be reasoned about by agents.
design-stance: Structures over drawings: every node has an id and may carry connectors and metadata.
Nestable: groups establish local coordinate systems; defs are reusable structures placed as instances.
Consistent transforms: translate, scale, and rotate compose; the runtime flattens to world-space points so rotation and scaling are exact.
Castle gate: documents may be written loosely; the runtime normalizes them to a canonical interior before use.
Agent-authorable: the shape is small, explicit, and JSON-friendly so coding agents can write it directly.

document-shape.description: Top-level object. Only `root` is required; everything else has defaults.

fields.version: Optional format version string. Defaults to "1".
fields.meta: Optional free-form object of metadata (title, notes, authorship). Not interpreted by the runtime.
fields.styles: Optional object mapping style-name -> style object. Referenced by nodes via a string `style`.
fields.defs: Optional object mapping def-name -> node. Reusable structures placed elsewhere by `instance` nodes.
fields.root: Required node. The top of the visible tree. If a bare primitive is given, the runtime wraps it in a group.
fields.connections: Optional list of connections (edges) joining two connectors. Rendered as wires that follow their endpoints.

connection-object.description: A durable relation joining two connectors. Endpoints are referenced by node path + connector name and resolved to world positions at render time, so a connection survives edits and follows its nodes. An endpoint that does not resolve simply does not draw.

fields.id: Optional stable identifier. Fabricated if absent.
fields.from: Endpoint object {node, name}: the editable node path and the connector/port name on it.
fields.to: Endpoint object {node, name}: the other end.
fields.style: Optional style name or inline style object. Defaults to a blue wire.

connection-object.editor-behavior: Dragging a port-bearing node so a connector lands within snap range of a compatible connector (output<->input, bidirectional<->any, anchor<->anchor) snaps it into alignment and records a connection. The snap-move and the connection commit as a single undoable action.

style-object.description: Concrete drawing properties. Defaults are applied at normalization.

fields.stroke: Outline/line color (hex string) or null for none. Default "#222222".
fields.fill: Fill color (hex string) or null for none. Default null.
fields.width: Stroke width in document units (number). Default 1. Scales with zoom.
fields.dash: Dash pattern as a string like "6 4" or a list of integers, or null. Default null.

style-object.resolution-order: Lowest to highest precedence: named style referenced by a node's string `style`, then an inline `style` object, then shorthand keys placed directly on the node (stroke, fill, width, dash).

common.type: Required. One of: group, rect, oval, line, polyline, port, instance, text.
common.id: Optional stable identifier. Fabricated if absent. Used for node paths, hit-testing, and editing.
common.style: Optional style name (string) or inline style object. May also use shorthand stroke/fill/width/dash keys directly on the node.

group.purpose: A container establishing a local coordinate system, holding child nodes and optional connectors.

fields.x, y: Translation of the local origin (alias tx, ty). Default 0.
fields.scale: Uniform scale (number). Or use sx, sy for per-axis. Default 1.
fields.rotate: Rotation in degrees, clockwise on screen. Default 0.
fields.connectors: Optional object mapping connector-name -> {x, y, direction, role} in local coordinates.

fields.closed: Optional bool; when true the path closes (and fills if a fill is set).

port.purpose: A connector point: a named attachment site drawn as a small marker. Ports make a structure attachable.

fields.name: Connector name (defaults to id).
fields.direction: Advisory: left, right, up, down, none. Default none.
fields.role: Advisory: input, output, bidirectional, anchor. Default anchor. Drives default marker color.
fields.radius: Marker radius in document units. Default 4.

instance.purpose: Place a reusable structure from `defs` with its own transform and id.

fields.connectors: Optional connectors specific to this placement.

text.purpose: A text label. Flagged experimental because tkinter Canvas text does not scale or rotate with full consistency. A future stroke-font built from these same primitives may replace it.

fields.size: Font size in document units. Default 12.
fields.anchor: tk anchor, e.g. nw, center. Default nw.

coordinate-system.units: Abstract document units. The runtime fits/zooms; units are not pixels.
coordinate-system.y-axis: Y grows downward, matching tkinter Canvas.
coordinate-system.transforms: A group's or instance's transform applies to its subtree. Application order on a point is rotate, then scale, then translate. Child world transform = compose(parent_world, child_local).
coordinate-system.rotation-note: The runtime renders rects and ovals as polygons of transformed points, so rotation is exact rather than relying on Canvas item rotation (which tkinter lacks).

runtime-contract.normalize: vectorloom.model.normalize_document(raw) -> canonical document. Raises VloomError on structural problems.
runtime-contract.render: vectorloom.render.render_document(canvas, doc, base_transform=IDENTITY) draws to a tkinter.Canvas and returns {items, by_item, connectors}.
runtime-contract.bounds: vectorloom.render.document_bounds(doc) -> (min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y) or None.
runtime-contract.export: vectorloom.export_png.render_image(doc, size) -> Pillow image; export_file(in, out) writes a PNG headlessly.
runtime-contract.node-path: render_document returns by_item mapping each canvas item id to a dotted node path (e.g. "root.launch=folder_node.body") plus resolved connector world positions, for hit-testing and editing.

open-questions: Formalize this format as a SoftSpec v3 specification with a data dictionary.
Decide whether text stays or is replaced by a stroke-font structure for full transform consistency.
Connector attachment semantics (accepts/emits typing, snap rules) for the editor.
